#### What's great

Long shows promise as a platform for creating and sharing projects, and I appreciate the concept and the effort behind it. However, after testing, I noticed several areas that could be improved.

Firstly, the UI isn’t particularly engaging, and the user flow can be confusing if care isn’t taken. While it’s functional, navigating between features isn’t intuitive, which may make the experience frustrating for new users. [...] 💼 Just explored Long—and it’s a breath of fresh air in the world of startup investing. It strips away the gatekeeping and hype that usually surrounds early-stage deals, and replaces it with something rare: access and fairness. What really stood out to me is the permissionless approach. Anyone, not just VCs or insiders, can support ambitious startups from day one. That’s a big shift—and honestly, a necessary one. More transparency, more opportunities, more people involved in shaping the next wave of innovation. The platform feels designed for those who believe in long-term impact rather than quick exits. It’s simple, clean, and focused on empowering both founders and early backers. That alignment of values is what makes Long special. We need more tools like this—ones that put power back in

results.title: Claude by Anthropic Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
Claude Sonnet 4.6 is the backbone of our entire platform, Humans.Team. Over 85 development sessions, Claude Code (powered by Sonnet) built 90% of our Next.js application — from Supabase database architecture and Row Level Security policies to AI journal integration, real-time notifications, PWA offline support, and a bilingual FR/EN system across 30+ pages.

What sets Sonnet 4.6 apart is its ability to hold deep context across long sessions. It remembers architectural decisions from hours ago, understands our codebase patterns, and writes production-ready TypeScript that rarely needs fixing. The reasoning is exceptional — it debugs complex issues by tracing through multiple files and connections. [...] long context windows (3)message limits (22)

Token burn can be reduced or the token count to be increased. Not everyone can afford the MAX plan even though that is the appropriate plan for heavy usage

#### vs Alternatives

Getting the best outcome with the same prompt across competitors is the best research. Gemini does not give visual outcomes nor does ChatGPT & the quality of Cursor outcome is lower compared to Claude Design

#### What's great

code generation (68)contextual understanding (51)creative writing (25)reasoning capabilities (46) [...] Based on 885 reviews

Review Claude by Anthropic?

Reviewers mostly see Claude as a strong tool for coding, long-form reasoning, and writing that sounds natural instead of generic. They repeatedly praise its ability to keep context across long chats, understand large codebases and documents, explain clearly, and sometimes push back rather than bluff. Founders of products like UXPin, IFTTT, and Wordware also highlight its production-ready output and agent workflows. Common complaints are strict message limits, weak image features, occasional refusals or hallucinations, and some rough app or UX issues.

results.title: Tool Finder - Find Productivity Tools Reviews (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
A Game-Changer for Discovering Productivity Tools Tool Finder is an incredibly useful platform that takes the guesswork out of finding the best productivity software. Its well-organized, user-friendly interface makes it easy to browse through a wide range of tools, from note-taking apps to project management solutions. What I love most is how it combines both expert insights and real user experiences, giving a balanced perspective on each tool’s strengths and weaknesses. The platform goes beyond simple recommendations by offering in-depth comparisons and practical guides that help users make informed decisions. Whether you’re a freelancer, a startup, or part of a larger team, Tool Finder saves you time by narrowing down the best options for your specific needs. The fact that it’s free to [...] Thanks so much Peter, means a lot

Finally found my go-to platform for software discovery! What sets Tool Finder apart is how well it understands different industry needs. The filtering system is spot-on, and I love how they embedded their YouTube videos for the tools so you get an in-depth review of the software! Brilliant platform that saves hours of research time!

Great platform for discovering productivity tools! What stands out is the curated video reviews and frequently updated content. The search functionality is excellent - helps you quickly find exactly what you need. With 100K+ monthly visitors, it's clearly becoming a go-to resource for software discovery. Really appreciate how it combines reviews, filtering, and instant search in one place. Makes the process of finding new tools much less overwhelming. The team behind it (Francesco D'Alessio and Karl Hadwen) brings solid expertise in the productivity space. Looking forward to seeing how the platform evolves! My current stack found through Tool Finder: Notion for notes, Morgen for calendar. Upvoted! 👍

results.title: Product Hunt Reviews (2026)
results.content:
We brought more than 4,000 real users, received over 150 thoughtful comments, and added hundreds of new followers. Yet only a small fraction of that showed up in the final score. Many of our users told us their comments or reviews never appeared publicly, even though they could see their posts when logged in. That was discouraging for people who took the time to register and give real feedback.

It increasingly feels like Product Hunt heavily weights long-time PH accounts while discounting thousands of real supporters who join for the first time. The result is that a smaller group of insiders can outweigh a much larger external community. That doesn’t feel like discovery anymore. [...] Support responses were generic and didn’t explain what actually happened. I even tried raising this in the forum, but the post never passed review despite being factual and non-abusive.

I still believe Product Hunt could be an important platform for makers, but it needs far more transparency about how votes, comments, and trust weighting work. Real users shouldn’t feel invisible. And founders shouldn’t leave launch day feeling confused about why genuine engagement doesn’t count.

Right now, it’s hard to tell whether Product Hunt is still serving makers — or slowly drifting away from what made it valuable.

Ruben Lozano
Lyssna

Lyssna

results.title: Drive reviews on Product Hunt
results.content:
Subscribe

1. Launch Guide
2. Two weeks post-launch

# Two weeks post-launch

Now that your launch is behind you, it’s time to focus on building social proof for your product. That includes driving users to leave reviews and touting your launch success with badges.

### This article covers:

ReviewsBadges & EmbedsLaunching your product againWhat did we miss?

## Reviews

Reviews are crucial to your long-term user acquisition strategy. Beyond launch day, millions of people who land on Product Hunt every week are searching for new things to try. Reviews of your product…

 Encourage people to try your product
 Help improve your conversion rate
 Increase your product visibility and credibility
 Improve your ranking on search engines

Product Hunt product review section [...] If you submit your product again within 6 months of your last launch, you’ll find you get a pop up message asking if your new launch is a major update or not. We've put this step in place due to feedback. Our community felt that the homepage was starting to look too homogeneous from multiple quick-fire launches from the same makers. A diluted homepage ultimately hurt the community by making it harder for people to discover new products. We aim for the homepage to remain a useful and authentic place for makers to launch and discover new products, and so this guideline was introduced.

Product Hunt significant update question
